name: Blackhawk Community
description_short: Blackhawk Community Credit Union is a Janesville, WI-based credit union offering comprehensive banking, lending, and investment services to members.
description_long:
Blackhawk Community Credit Union serves the Janesville, Wisconsin community and surrounding service areas as a full-service credit union. The organization provides traditional banking services alongside lending products and financial planning to enhance member financial well-being. As a credit union rather than a commercial bank, it operates on a member-owned, not-for-profit basis.

The institution offers a broad range of financial products including high-yield checking and savings accounts, flexible auto loans, multiple mortgage options, business loans, and investment/retirement planning services. On the savings side, members can access regular share savings accounts (65,770 active accounts according to NCUA data), money market accounts ($9.06M in assets across 350 accounts), share certificates/CDs (5,525 active accounts), and IRA/KEOGH retirement accounts (1,517 active accounts). Mortgage lending is a significant focus, with offerings including fixed-rate mortgages (15+ years and under 15 years), adjustable-rate mortgages, balloon/hybrid options, and a first-time homebuyer program.

Blackhawk Community distinguishes itself through personalized service, streamlined mortgage processing with fast approvals, dedicated financial planning and wealth management, robust online and mobile banking capabilities, and exclusive member benefits and discounts. The credit union positions itself as a relationship-focused alternative to larger institutions, emphasizing community focus and member-centric financial solutions.

As a credit union, Blackhawk Community is subject to NCUA oversight and member governance. The website provides limited specific information about interest rates, loan terms, membership requirements, or fees. The profile is based on marketing content without independent verification of current rates, processing times, or member satisfaction metrics. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Share certificates/CDs with low minimum balances
Fixed-rate mortgages (15+ years and under 15 years)
Balloon and hybrid mortgages (5 years or less)
Mortgage calculator and loan calculator tools
pros: Credit union structure offers potential for better rates and lower fees than traditional banks
Diverse mortgage options including first-time homebuyer programs with low down payments and educational resources
Strong deposit base documented by NCUA (65,770+ savings accounts, $9M+ in money market deposits)
Multiple account types including high-yield checking, savings, money market, CDs, and IRAs
Streamlined mortgage processing with fast approvals and stress-free transactions claimed
Online and mobile banking capabilities for convenient account management
Offers business loans and financial planning services beyond basic consumer banking
cons: Website contains minimal specific information about current interest rates, APRs, or fee schedules
No documented membership eligibility requirements or how to join the credit union
Limited transparency on loan approval criteria, processing timelines, or minimum qualifications
No customer reviews, ratings, or satisfaction data provided on the website
Geographic limitation to Janesville, WI area may restrict access for out-of-area members
best_for: Janesville, WI residents seeking a community-focused alternative to national banks
First-time homebuyers looking for educational resources and flexible down payment options
Local small business owners needing relationship-based lending and business financial services
Members prioritizing personalized financial planning and wealth management over self-service banking
diagnosis: Blackhawk Community Credit Union is best for Janesville-area residents and local business owners seeking personalized, relationship-based banking with competitive credit union rates and comprehensive lending options. Primary caveat: the website lacks essential details on current rates, fees, membership requirements, and approval criteria—prospective members should contact the credit union directly for specific terms and eligibility information.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
